text: Discovered black mold in the master bedroom closet on Oct 12th. It covers approximately 3 square feet of the lower drywall.
description: Initial discovery of the mold issue.

text: Maintenance from Essex Management arrived on Oct 15th, sprayed bleach on the wall, and left. They did not fix the underlying leak from the roof.
description: Inadequate repair attempt by the landlord.

text: Sent formal written notice to Essex Property Management via Certified Mail detailing the ongoing mold issue and requesting professional remediation within 14 days.
Received email from property manager 'Sarah' at Essex blaming condensation and declining to send a licensed mold inspector.
Visited urgent care on Oct 20th for severe respiratory irritation and chronic coughing. Doctor noted potential environmental mold exposure as cause.
description: Medical record linking symptoms to the apartment environment.

text: Under the State's Implied Warranty of Habitability, the landlord is legally mandated to provide a safe, livable environment, which explicitly includes freedom from toxic mold.
Civil Code Section 1941.1 dictates that if a landlord fails to repair dilapidations affecting habitability, a tenant may utilize 'repair and deduct' or vacate the premises (Constructive Eviction).
Task: Hire a private certified mold inspector to run an air quality test to establish an independent evidentiary baseline.
Task: Draft a Constructive Eviction notice to break the lease without penalty, citing the unlivable conditions and documented health impacts.
description: Final legal step to exit the lease.
